description: Bitbucket Data Center REST API 1.0 (plus build-status/1.0, default-reviewers/1.0, required-builds/latest, jira/1.0, insights/1.0). Bearer PAT auth via PasswordSafe. All calls go through BitbucketBranchClient in :core; no per-module client duplication. DC edition confirmed via /rest/capabilities 'repository-mirroring' capability.

endpoints.summary: Server version, build number and display name. Mirrors Jira's /serverInfo. Use for settings validation and DC edition detection.
gotchas: Prefer /rest/capabilities for edition detection — repository-mirroring capability is DC-exclusive and works without admin scope

endpoints.summary: Capability map listing installed plugins (build, deployment, code-insights, default-reviewers, jira-link, repository-mirroring, webhooks). Use to gate optional endpoint adoption.
gotchas: repository-mirroring presence confirms Data Center edition without requiring admin token
Code Insights, Jira-link, default-reviewers capabilities must be confirmed before adopting those sub-API endpoints

endpoints.summary: List all projects visible to the authenticated user. Returns key, name, description per project.

params.name: limit
params.description: Max results per page (default 25, used as 100 in plugin)

gotchas: Returns paginated results; isLastPage=false when more pages remain — plugin hardcodes limit=100 and does not paginate beyond page 1

endpoints.summary: List repositories in a project. Returns slug, name, description, scmId per repo.

verdict.classification: R-INV: probed, not yet called by plugin code
verdict.reasoning: Confirmed 200 on DC 9.4.16. Useful for agent's bitbucket_repo list_repos action and the repo picker flow.

endpoints.summary: Get repository metadata including server-canonical clone URLs (links.clone). Used by auto-detection to validate remote URLs.
gotchas: links.clone[] contains both http and ssh entries — plugin resolves https entry by name='http'
404 = repo not found (wrong projectKey/repoSlug)

endpoints.summary: Get the default branch of a repository. Returns branch id, displayId, latestCommit.
endpoints.provenance: probe Result_Bitbucket/bundle-full-sweep.unpacked/summary.md existing table row 5; raw/repo_default_branch.json; BitbucketBranchClient.kt:1020-1050

endpoints.summary: List branches sorted by modification date. With details=true (R-SWAP-3) returns metadata per branch including aheadBehind, latestCommit info, jira issues and build state — eliminating per-branch follow-up calls.

params.name: details
params.description: When true, includes metadata per branch (aheadBehind, latestCommit author, jiraIssues, build). R-SWAP-3 from 2026-05-07 audit — eliminates N follow-up calls.

params.name: filterText
params.description: Case-insensitive prefix filter on branch displayId

gotchas: filterText is added via OkHttp addQueryParameter to prevent injection when branch name contains & or = characters
metadata provider keys are long dotted strings; decode each key on demand — the set varies by installed plugins

endpoints.summary: Create a new branch from a start point (SHA or ref). Returns the created branch object.
gotchas: Requires Repository Write (or Admin) permission — 401/403 returned otherwise
409 = branch already exists
startPoint can be a full SHA or refs/heads/<name>

endpoints.summary: Get raw file content at a specific ref. Returns plain text when ?raw query param is present.

params.name: filePath
params.description: File path within the repo (NOT URL-encoded; literal slashes are preserved)

params.name: at
params.description: Branch ref or SHA to read file at

params.name: raw
params.description: Presence of this param (no value) returns raw file bytes instead of JSON

gotchas: filePath must NOT be URL-encoded — Bitbucket expects literal path separators in the URL
Returns text/plain, not JSON — plugin sends Accept: text/plain
404 if file does not exist at the specified ref

endpoints.summary: Search users by filter text. Matches case-insensitively across username (name), displayName, and emailAddress. With permission.1=REPO_READ params, restricts results to users who have effective read access on the target repo.

params.name: filter
params.description: Filter string matched against name, displayName, emailAddress

params.name: permission.1
params.description: When set to REPO_READ alongside permission.1.projectKey and permission.1.repositorySlug, restricts results to users with effective read access on that repo (honours group-expanded permissions)

params.name: permission.1.projectKey
params.description: Project key for repo-scoped permission filter

params.name: permission.1.repositorySlug
params.description: Repository slug for repo-scoped permission filter

gotchas: The permission.1=REPO_READ variant mirrors the Bitbucket web UI reviewer picker and honours group-expanded permissions, unlike /repos/{r}/permissions/users which only covers direct grants
CreatePrDialog uses the repo-scoped variant; the existing-PR Add Reviewer popup was missing the repo scope until the 2026-05-07 write-ops audit fix

endpoints.summary: Returns the plain-text username of the currently authenticated user. Used to identify the current user for role-filter queries.
gotchas: Returns plain text (not JSON) — do not parse as JSON
Not a REST API endpoint; lives under /plugins/servlet/

endpoints.summary: Recently-touched repositories for the calling user. Useful for ranking the repo picker by recency-for-me instead of alphabetical.

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed 200 on DC 9.4.16. Candidate for repo picker UX improvement.

endpoints.summary: Fetch all default-reviewer conditions for a repo. Each condition has sourceRefMatcher, targetRefMatcher, reviewers list and requiredApprovals. Gated on default-reviewers plugin installation (404 if not installed).
gotchas: 404 if the default-reviewers plugin is not installed — treat as empty list, not a failure
Matcher type discriminator is the nested type.id field (BRANCH, MODEL_BRANCH, MODEL_CATEGORY, ANY_REF, PATTERN) — not the top-level id string
Plugin's getDefaultReviewers() unions ALL conditions; getDefaultReviewersForBranch() filters by sourceRefMatcher AND targetRefMatcher to match web UI behaviour (2026-05-07 audit P1 fix #6)

endpoints.summary: List pull requests for a repo. Supports state, role, direction, at (branch ref) filtering. Paginated. Used for per-repo PR listing (AUTHOR/REVIEWER roles and all-open variants).

params.name: state
params.description: Filter by PR state: OPEN, MERGED, DECLINED, ALL

params.name: role.1
params.description: Filter by current user's role: AUTHOR or REVIEWER

params.name: username.1
params.description: Username to filter by when role.1 is set

params.name: direction
params.description: OUTGOING for PRs from a branch, INCOMING for PRs targeting a branch

params.name: at
params.description: Branch ref to filter by (used with direction=OUTGOING to check if a PR already exists for a branch)

params.name: limit
params.description: Max results per page (plugin uses 25 or 100)

gotchas: direction=OUTGOING + at=refs/heads/{branch} is the 'is there already a PR for this branch' check — plugin uses this before offering Create PR
role.1=AUTHOR/REVIEWER returns per-repo results; for cross-repo use the dashboard endpoint (R-SWAP-1/2)

endpoints.summary: Cross-repo PR dashboard. Collapses N per-repo calls into 1 by returning PRs across all repos the user has access to. Replaces per-repo AUTHOR/REVIEWER iteration (R-SWAP-1 and R-SWAP-2 from 2026-05-07 audit). Returns toRef.repository for repo identification.
gotchas: fromRef and toRef carry repository.slug + repository.project.key on dashboard results — use these to bucket PRs per repo

endpoints.summary: Get full details of a specific pull request including title, description, state, version (for optimistic locking), reviewers, fromRef, toRef, author.
gotchas: version field is required for all PUT/POST mutation endpoints — always fetch fresh before mutating
modifyPullRequest() and mergePullRequestWithRetry() implement fetch-modify-write with one 409 retry to handle concurrent edits

endpoints.summary: Activity timeline for a PR: COMMENTED, APPROVED, UNAPPROVED, REVIEWED, DECLINED, MERGED, RESCOPED, OPENED events. Also used as the canonical source for PR comments on DC 9.4 (direct /comments returns 400 — see gotchas).
gotchas: DC 9.4: GET /pull-requests/{id}/comments without path= or count=true returns 400 — the activities timeline is the correct replacement for listing all PR comments
Plugin paginator aggregates up to 20 pages (safety cap of ~1000 activities) before returning with isLastPage=false indicating truncation
listPrComments() in the plugin filters action=COMMENTED from activities and maps to BitbucketPrCommentResponse — some fields (severity, state, thread replies) are absent from the activity payload

endpoints.summary: Full unified diff for a PR as plain text. Capped at 327,680 chars in the plugin.
gotchas: Returns text/plain unified diff — plugin sends Accept: text/plain
Large PRs can be megabytes — plugin caps at MAX_DIFF_CHARS=327680 chars and appends a truncation notice

endpoints.summary: Per-file scoped diff. Smaller payload than the full /diff endpoint for large PRs. Supports contextLines query param.

params.name: filePath
params.description: File path within the PR to scope the diff to

params.name: contextLines
params.description: Number of context lines around each hunk

endpoints.provenance: probe Result_Bitbucket/bundle-full-sweep.unpacked/raw/pr_diff_filescoped.json (200, 274ms); summary.md swap table R-ADD-14; research/2026-05-07-bitbucket-recommendations.md §4 R-ADD-14

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed 200 on DC 9.4.16. Candidate swap for huge PRs to fetch diff per file instead of the monolithic /diff.

gotchas: Same plain-text response shape as /diff but scoped to one file

endpoints.summary: List files changed in a PR (paginated). Returns path, srcPath, type (ADD/MODIFY/DELETE/COPY/MOVE) and nodeType per change.
gotchas: Plugin aggregates up to 20 pages (safety cap of 2000 files) before returning with isLastPage=false indicating truncation

endpoints.summary: List commits included in a PR. Returns commit id, displayId, message, author, authorTimestamp.
Check merge preconditions. Returns canMerge, conflicted, outcome, and vetoes list with human-readable messages.
gotchas: vetoes[] may contain 'Not all required builds are successful yet', 'Requires approvals', 'Not all required reviewers have approved yet', 'Merge vetoed: must rebase' — all confirmed on DC 9.4.16

endpoints.summary: Explicit participants endpoint with role, approved, status (UNAPPROVED/APPROVED/NEEDS_WORK) and lastReviewedCommit per reviewer. Richer than the embedded reviewers array on getPullRequest (R-SWAP-5).

Blocker-severity comments on a PR. With count=true returns just {size} for cheap badge counters. Without count=true returns the values list. Replaces client-side filtering of the full comments list (R-SWAP-4).

params.name: count
params.description: When true, returns only {size} — faster for badge display

endpoints.summary: Inbox PR count — returns {count: N} integer. Cheap endpoint for tool-window unread badge.
endpoints.provenance: probe Result_Bitbucket/bundle-full-sweep.unpacked/raw/inbox_pr_count.json; summary.md swap table R-ADD-1; research/2026-05-07-bitbucket-recommendations.md §4 R-ADD-1

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed 200 on DC 9.4.16. Candidate for a tool-window PR inbox badge.

endpoints.summary: PRs requiring the current user's action (Atlassian's algorithmic inbox). More targeted than role=REVIEWER dashboard.
endpoints.provenance: probe Result_Bitbucket/bundle-full-sweep.unpacked/raw/inbox_pr_list.json; summary.md swap table R-ADD-2; research/2026-05-07-bitbucket-recommendations.md §4 R-ADD-2

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed 200 on DC 9.4.16. Candidate for a 'PRs needing your attention' panel.

endpoints.summary: Create a pull request. Returns the created PR including id and links.self.
gotchas: fromRef and toRef only need {id} for same-repo PRs — the server fills repository from the URL path
Cross-fork PRs require full repository context in fromRef/toRef — not supported by this plugin
409 = PR already exists for that from/to branch combination
reviewers[].user.name must be the Bitbucket username (not displayName)

endpoints.summary: Update PR title, description, and reviewers. Requires current version in body for optimistic locking. Returns updated PR detail.
endpoints.provenance: BitbucketBranchClient.kt:1617-1655; probe summary.md writes-inventoried table; research/2026-05-07-bitbucket-pr-write-ops-audit.md §3 P0
gotchas: 409 = stale version — another user modified the PR between GET and PUT. Plugin's modifyPullRequest() wraps this with one automatic retry on 409
Always fetch fresh PR detail to get the current version before calling PUT — never cache the version across user interactions

endpoints.summary: Merge a PR. Version is required as a query parameter. Optional body: strategyId, deleteSourceRef, message.

params.name: version
params.description: Current PR version for optimistic locking

endpoints.provenance: BitbucketBranchClient.kt:1951-2018; probe summary.md writes-inventoried table; research/2026-05-07-bitbucket-pr-write-ops-audit.md §3 P1
gotchas: 409 with PullRequestOutOfDateException in body = stale version (retry with fresh version). 409 with other exceptionName = merge veto (don't retry). Plugin distinguishes by parsing errors[].exceptionName
mergePullRequestWithRetry() implements fetch-version + merge POST with one 409 retry for the stale-version case
GET /merge (without POST body) is the pre-flight check for canMerge — always call that first to surface veto messages before showing the merge dialog

endpoints.summary: Decline a PR. Version is a required query parameter.

params.name: version
params.description: Current PR version for optimistic locking

endpoints.requestBody: Empty JSON body (send empty string with Content-Type: application/json)
gotchas: 409 = stale version — refresh PR and retry

endpoints.summary: Approve a PR as the current user. Returns the reviewer object with approved=true.
endpoints.requestBody: Empty JSON body (send empty string with Content-Type: application/json)
gotchas: 409 = already approved or not a reviewer on this PR

endpoints.summary: Remove (unapprove) a PR approval by the current user.
Get repo-level merge strategy settings. Returns defaultStrategy and list of enabled strategies. On 404 (repo has no per-repo override) falls back to the project-level equivalent endpoint.
endpoints.provenance: BitbucketBranchClient.kt:2175-2237; probe Result_Bitbucket/bundle-full-sweep.unpacked/summary.md existing table (404 on test repo); research/2026-05-07-bitbucket-recommendations.md §1.2
gotchas: 404 means the repo has no per-repo override — must fall back to GET /rest/api/1.0/projects/{p}/settings/pull-requests/git
Plugin caches the resolution per (projectKey, repoSlug) in-memory so subsequent calls skip the 404
Both repo and project URLs return the same BitbucketRepoSettingsResponse shape

endpoints.summary: Add a comment, inline (file/line) comment, or reply to an existing comment on a PR. All three variants use the same endpoint with different body shapes.
endpoints.provenance: BitbucketBranchClient.kt:1825-1862, 2598-2656, 2662-2702; probe summary.md writes-inventoried table; research/2026-05-07-bitbucket-pr-write-ops-audit.md §3; research/2026-05-07-bitbucket-recommendations.md §4 R-ADD-7 (inline audit)
gotchas: diffType=COMMIT + fromHash + toHash pins the inline comment to the specific diff range so it doesn't float when new commits land. Omitting these three fields gives server-default EFFECTIVE behaviour
For AI reviews, capture toRef.latestCommit at review time and pass as toHash with diffType=COMMIT (2026-05-07 audit P1 fix #7)
lineType: ADDED, REMOVED, or CONTEXT. fileType: TO for new/added lines, FROM for removed lines. Plugin derives fileType automatically from lineType

endpoints.summary: Get a single comment by ID. Returns full comment detail including version (needed for edit/delete), text, author, anchor, state.
gotchas: permittedOperations tells whether the authenticated user can edit/delete/transition this comment
version is required for subsequent PUT (edit) or DELETE calls

endpoints.summary: Edit a comment (update text) or change its state (RESOLVED/OPEN). Both operations use the same endpoint path; the body shape differs. Version is required for optimistic locking.
gotchas: 409 = stale version (STALE_VERSION) — re-fetch comment and retry
Plugin's resolvePrComment() and reopenPrComment() both route through setCommentState() which uses this same PUT path with {state: 'RESOLVED'} or {state: 'OPEN'}

endpoints.summary: Delete a comment. Version is a required query parameter for optimistic locking.
gotchas: 409 = stale version — re-fetch and retry

endpoints.summary: Set reviewer status for a named participant: APPROVED, NEEDS_WORK, or UNAPPROVED. Body includes status and approved fields.
gotchas: 409 = participant not found on this PR or status transition invalid
approved field must mirror status: true when status=APPROVED, false otherwise

endpoints.summary: List build statuses for a commit SHA. Returns {state, key, name, url, description, dateAdded} per build. Used for PR build-status badge.
gotchas: key field is typically the Bamboo build key including the build number (e.g. PROJ-PLAN-42). Plugin's extractPlanKey() strips the trailing numeric build number to get the plan key for triggering new builds
404 = no builds for this commit — treated as empty list, not an error
Rich endpoint GET /rest/api/latest/.../commits/{cid}/builds returned 400 on this org's DC instance because Bamboo agents publish only basic v1 schema — keep v1 as the long-term build-status fetch

endpoints.summary: Cheap aggregate build counter for a commit: {successful, failed, inProgress, cancelled, unknown}. More efficient than listing all build statuses when only counts are needed.
Reverse lookup: given a commit SHA, return all PRs that contain that commit. Powers the Bamboo→PR notification bridge — when a build fails for commit X, look up the affected PR authors.
gotchas: Probe returned size=0 on the test SHA (schema validated, correct shape) — endpoint is live even when no PRs contain the commit

endpoints.summary: Jira issues linked to a PR via the Atlassian Jira-link plugin. Returns [{key, url}] per linked issue. Replaces the plugin's previous manual regex extraction over PR title, branch name and commit messages.
gotchas: 404 = Jira-link plugin not installed — treat as empty list, not a failure
Requires Jira-link plugin (capability 'dev-status' confirmed installed on this instance via /rest/capabilities)

endpoints.summary: Required-builds merge-check conditions for a repo. Returns {buildParentKeys, refMatcher, exemptRefMatcher} per condition. Used to display 'required builds before merge' indicators on PRs.
gotchas: Use /rest/required-builds/latest/ base path — NOT /rest/api/1.0/ which 404s on DC 9.4
404 = required-builds plugin not installed — treated as empty list, not a failure
Empty values[] is a valid response meaning no required builds are configured for this repo

endpoints.summary: Code Insights reports for a commit (Sonar, SCA tools publish here). Returns list of reports with reporter, result, data.
endpoints.provenance: probe Result_Bitbucket/bundle-full-sweep.unpacked/raw/insights_reports.json (200, empty — no reports published on test commit); summary.md feature table R-ADD-9; research/2026-05-07-bitbucket-recommendations.md §4 R-ADD-9

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed 200 on DC 9.4.16. Code Insights capability confirmed installed. Empty on test commit but endpoint and schema are valid. Candidate for Quality tab integration.

gotchas: Gated on Code Insights plugin — check capabilities before adopting
Base path /rest/insights/1.0/ confirmed on this instance via /rest/insights/latest/capabilities endpoint

endpoints.summary: All Code Insights annotations on a commit across all reports. Supports optional key, path, severity, type filter params.
endpoints.provenance: probe Result_Bitbucket/bundle-full-sweep.unpacked/raw/insights_annotations_all.json (200, empty); summary.md feature table R-ADD-10; research/2026-05-07-bitbucket-recommendations.md §4 R-ADD-10

verdict.reasoning: Confirmed 200 on DC 9.4.16. Would power inline-on-diff overlays for Sonar/SCA findings.

gotchas: Gated on Code Insights plugin

endpoints.summary: List all PR comments (root threads). Returns 400 on DC 9.4 without required path= or count=true parameter. Plugin migrated to using /activities and filtering action=COMMENTED.

params.name: path
params.description: Required on DC 9.4 to list file-path-scoped comments; omitting it causes 400

params.name: count
params.description: When true, returns only size counter (also works on DC 9.4 without path)

verdict.classification: Broken on DC 9.4: requires path= or count=true parameter
verdict.reasoning: DC 9.4's /comments listing requires either path (for path-anchored comments) or count=true. Listing 'all root threads in a PR' without those parameters is rejected. Plugin replaced this call with /activities filtering.

gotchas: This endpoint WORKED on older DC versions — the rejection is a DC 9.4 behaviour change
